rationale_overall: No changes this quarter. The current watchlist [NVDA, URA, RKLB, IONQ, XLE] is at cap=5 and already places exactly one bet on each of the four profile-named waves (AI=NVDA, nuclear=URA, rockets=RKLB, quantum=IONQ) plus the one named non-tech wave (geopolitical-energy=XLE). Every position has a fresh, in-window, pre-2022-07-01 dated catalyst that confirms the underlying wave thesis: (a) NVDA reported Q1 FY23 on May 25, 2022 with record $8.29B revenue (+46% YoY) and Data Center revenue of $3.75B (+83% YoY, +15% sequential) — the AI-compute leg of the thesis is accelerating even as the consumer-gaming leg softens, and the profile is the AI wave, not the consumer-GPU cycle; (b) the uranium spot price hit US$64.61/lb in mid-April 2022, an 11-year high, on Rosatom sanction fears and Kazakh civil-unrest residual supply concerns, with utilities returning to long-term contracting — URA's nuclear-fuel thesis is intact and strengthening; (c) Rocket Lab successfully launched NASA's CAPSTONE CubeSat to a lunar near-rectilinear halo orbit on June 28, 2022 using Electron + the new Lunar Photon upper stage — Electron's first deep-space mission, a dated thesis validator three days before the as-of date; (d) XLE: Brent and WTI sustained ~$110+/bbl through most of Q2 2022 on the unresolved Russia-Ukraine geopolitical premium, and the EIA's full-year 2022 averages (Brent $100/b, WTI $95/b) confirm the structural repricing — the geopolitical-energy wave is mid-cycle, not crested. The one borderline name is IONQ, which absorbed a Scorpion Capital short report on May 3, 2022 alleging revenue/technology misrepresentation, drove a ~10% same-day decline, and contributed to a ~70% YTD drawdown to $5.23 by late June. However: (i) IonQ's co-founders published a detailed point-by-point public rebuttal on May 12, 2022 noting Scorpion's own disclosed short position and admission to selectively editing interview transcripts; (ii) Scorpion's allegations are about revenue quality, not about the underlying trapped-ion quantum technology, and the profile's bet is on the quantum WAVE not on any specific revenue trajectory; (iii) IONQ remains the only credible US-listed pure-play quantum name at this as-of date — Rigetti (RGTI) only began trading on Mar 2, 2022 via SPAC and faces the same pre-revenue-SPAC volatility profile without solving any of the concerns Scorpion raised, while D-Wave's SPAC merger had not closed pre-2022-07-01 (it closed Aug 5, 2022 as QBTS) so QBTS fails the pre-date listing guardrail; (iv) the curator's job is wave curation, not within-wave stock-picking, and rotating one volatile pre-revenue quantum SPAC for another would be a cosmetic name-rotation against an unchanged wave thesis. The macro backdrop (Fed 75bp hike on June 15, 2022 — pre-date and already in the pre-date suppression list; recession concerns emerging in second-half June) is a valuation-multiple headwind across the whole watchlist, not a wave-thesis change, so it does not justify any single swap. The profile excludes engineered_biology, the profile's named non-tech waves are geopolitical-energy (filled by XLE) and demographics (no clean wave-pure-play exists at this as-of date in a way that would beat the current five), and defense — though it has a real Q2 2022 catalyst in the $309M Lockheed/Raytheon Javelin backfill contract and the May 27 Stinger backfill — is not a profile-named wave, so it does not get a slot at cap=5. The disciplined call is to hold.
